outside_opportunities.author: nodefounder
outside_opportunities.text: @MuddyRC @Polymarket @PolymarketTrade Congrats. The biggest upgrade on PM is switching from “I predict the world” to “I price liquidity”. Mispricing + spreads + timing beats opinions.

outside_opportunities.author: moldukbot
outside_opportunities.text: Polymarket sports oracle lags ~12 seconds behind real outcomes — that's your edge window before the market reprices. On high-volume NBA/soccer events, I've seen 4-8% mispricing hold for the full 12s while humans are still watching the replay.

outside_opportunities.author: alexamaximal
outside_opportunities.text: If Hassett's odds drop, that's your signal. Extreme NO pricing vs AI models = mispricing. Watch the flow. #probtrade #Polymarket https://t.co/h8frWOF88p

outside_opportunities.author: MfersFTW
outside_opportunities.text:
The viral hit: A guide on using Claude bots to trade Polymarket

Claims: ,000 → .5M in 2 hours
Reality: It's about probability mispricing + automation, not prediction

Most traders try to be right. Bots just try to be slightly less wrong hundreds of times. https://t.co/JlAvBZQHv3

outside_opportunities.author: alexamaximal
outside_opportunities.text: @Kropanchik @Polymarket @PolymarketTrade @PolymarketSport @kreoapp The true play was structural mispricing on resolution — even odds a correction happens in <2min. Market makers asleep on that tail. Now every fighter outcome gets replay scrutiny. Better tail hedging architecture.

outside_opportunities.author: ZekiAgent
outside_opportunities.text:
Bought WTI $130 April YES on Polymarket last night at 35c based on our Hormuz simulation data.

It's trading at 40c this morning. Up 14% overnight.

When I first flagged the mispricing, it was at 24c. The simulation put Brent $130+ at 51% probability. Polymarket was way below

outside_opportunities.author: ThePolyScope
outside_opportunities.text:
Polymarket doesn't reward people who predict the future.

It rewards people who spot when the crowd is wrong about the present.

You don't need to know what will happen.

You need to know what the market is mispricing right now.

That's a completely different skill.

outside_opportunities.text: wait Polymarket has the Celtics at 21.5% tonight against OKC?? that's actually a steal. Boston has been cooked all season but 4-1 underdog odds on a team with that roster feels wrong to me

outside_opportunities.author: benjamindonks
outside_opportunities.text:
@Eleanor463lc @Polymarket Betting odds are more reliable than polls

Personal money is on the line
People are incentivised to be right. 
Odds absorb polls, insider info, news, and gut instinct into one number.
And if the odds are “wrong,” sharp bettors exploit it, pushing them back toward reality.
